task: Make the market dashboard super cool and unique. Take inspiration from https://kindred-glow-create.lovable.app/pulse — modern, glowing, animated, premium feel with bold typography, gradients, motion, and live-data feel.

stages.spec:
## Pulse — premium dark market dashboard ### Why this will be good Today `/pulse` is a competent light-themed dashboard that feels like a report. The ambition is to make it feel like a live trading terminal for Dubai real estate — dark, glowing, alive, with numbers that animate in, charts that reveal on scroll, and a gold-on-emerald palette that signals premium. The pulse theme tokens already exist in `globals.css` and only need to be put to work. Production-ready means: dark hero seamlessly continues into dark body (no white flash), every chart re-styled for dark with branded fills + dark tooltips, KPIs count up on viewport entry, empty/loading states get a shimmer treatment so the cold-start API call feels intentional rather than broken, mobile holds at 390px with all tap targets ≥44px. Benchmarks: **Stripe Atlas analytics** (dark, restrained, numbers-first), **Linear Insights** (motion that explains causality), **Plaid dashboard** (data density without clutter), and the user-supplied **kindred-glow pulse reference** for the gold-glow vocabulary. ### Demo path 1. Visit `/pulse` — green→dark hero already exists; below it the page body is now also dark, no transition flash. 2. As you scroll, KPI cards fade up and numbers tick from 0 to final value once. 3. Chart bars draw in left-to-right; hovering a bar shows a dark tooltip with a thin gold border. 4. Switch chart tabs (Price / Yield / Volume / Bedroom) — active tab has a soft gold glow; chart re-renders with brand-coloured bars. 5. Sort the community table — selected row gets a gold left-edge accent. 6. Visit homepage, scroll to MarketDashboard — it now reads as a self-contained dark island that previews the same vocabulary as `/pulse`, inviting click-through. ### Primary target `src/app/pulse/PulsePageClient.tsx` (~585 lines, currently light-themed). ### Secondary target `src/components/MarketDashboard.tsx` (homepage section). Apply the same dark-island treatment so it feels like a teaser for `/pulse`, not a separate UI. ### Read-only — do not modify - `src/app/[locale]/pulse/page.tsx` (server, owns the green→dark hero gradient already) - `src/app/globals.css` (all `.pulse-*` tokens and classes already defined) - Translation JSON files (no new keys; namespace stays `pulse` and `marketDashboard`) - API routes --- ### What this surface must achieve (intent, not pixels) DEV: read the existing file, then apply these intents. Use the existing `--pulse-*` tokens and the `.pulse-dark`, `.pulse-glass`, `.pulse-glass-hover`, `.pulse-gold-shimmer`, `.pulse-gradient-text` classes. Do NOT introduce new tokens, new packages, or new colour values. Make pixel decisions (exact sizes, gaps, shadow depths, easing curves) yourself — you have the rendered UI; this brief does not. **1. Dark island, seamless from hero.** Wrap the PulsePageClient body so the entire page below the server-rendered hero sits on `--pulse-bg`. The transition from the green hero into the dark body should feel intentional — no white flash, no contrast cliff. Apply the `.pulse-dark` class on the outer wrapper so semantic tokens (`bg-card`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`) cascade to dark values automatically. This single move handles ~80% of theming; only override per-element where the cascade misses. **2. KPI cards — alive, not static.** Four headline KPIs at the top must read as *the* signature element. Glass surface (`.pulse-glass`), oversized numerals, tiny uppercase label above. The number must count up from 0 to its final value the first time the card enters the viewport — use `framer-motion`'s `useMotionValue` + `useTransform` + `animate()`, gated by `useInView({ once: true })`. Decimals (yield %) and large integers (listings) both need to read correctly during the count. On hover, a soft gold glow should appear (the kind that suggests "this is live data"). One of the four cards should be visually accented with the gold token to act as the hero metric — pick the one with the strongest narrative (avg yield or total listings, your call). Use the existing `.pulse-gradient-text` class for that hero number if it improves the read. **3. Charts — dark restyle, branded fills, motion on entry.** Every Recharts chart in the file (BarChart, AreaChart, LineChart, RadarChart) must read as dark-native: - Container surface uses the pulse surface token. - `CartesianGrid` and `PolarGrid` strokes use `--pulse-border`. - Axis ticks use `--pulse-text-muted`. - Bar/Area/Line fills come from the existing brand palette: green family (`--pulse-green`, `--pulse-green-light`) for volume/price/count, gold (`--pulse-gold`) for yield. Pick which series gets gold based on what the section is about — gold is the "return / value" colour throughout. - Tooltip is dark-styled with a thin gold border, small radius, ≤12px text. The tooltip must feel like part of the dark UI, not a Recharts default popping in. - Recharts `isAnimationActive` is fine for the on-entry draw; don't fight it. - **Critical bug to fix:** the chart-tab map currently uses a variable named `t` which shadows the i18n `t()` function. Rename to `tab` (or similar) when re-styling tabs. **4. Chart tab bar.** Active tab uses a soft gold pill (`bg-[--pulse-gold]/0.15` family with a gold border tint and gold text). Inactive tabs are muted text on transparent, with a subtle hover wash from `--pulse-surface-hover`. The active state should read at a glance — the user must never wonder which tab is selected. **5. Section headers.** Existing `SectionHeader` already shows a small label + heading. Re-tone for dark: gold accent line and label, light heading text, and add a thin animated gold underline that draws in from `scaleX: 0 → 1` when the header enters the viewport. Use this as the rhythm device that makes the page feel composed rather than scrolled. **6. Investment-pick / community-matrix cards.** Selected card: gold border + faint gold-tinted surface. Hover (un-selected): border lifts toward the gold-hover token. ScoreBadges (high/mid/low) currently use `emerald-100`/`amber-100` Tailwind classes — re-tone to pulse-green / pulse-gold / pulse-surface families so they feel native to the dark surface. **7. Community table.** Dark surface, header row sits a shade lower than body rows so the head reads as a header. Row hover uses the surface-hover token. Selected row gets a `2px` gold left edge and a faint gold tint — this is the "you are here" affordance. Yield numbers colour-code by tier (high / mid / low) using the pulse green-light, gold, and muted tokens respectively. Progress bars (e.g. listing counts) use pulse-green on a pulse-border track. **8. Currency converter.** `.pulse-glass` card. Inputs use the dark bg token with a gold focus ring. Currency tiles (USD/EUR/GBP/...) sit on the dark bg with a thin pulse-border, hover to gold-hover border. Numbers always retain the existing thousand-separator formatting. **9. Economic indicators & news.** Indicator cards: same dark glass pattern as KPIs but smaller. News cards: `.pulse-glass` + `.pulse-glass-hover`, source badge in the gold token family, title hovers to gold. Image thumbnails should sit cleanly on the dark surface (no white halo from un-styled `` borders). **10. Empty / loading states are features here, not afterthoughts.** The DLD transactions API and the Render `/api/market-data` endpoint can cold-start (up to ~15s). The empty state must NOT feel broken — use a dark skeleton shimmer (subtle gradient sweeping across `--pulse-surface` blocks shaped like the eventual KPIs and chart) so the user perceives "loading" not "nothing here." The DLD permanent empty state (when the feature is intentionally disabled) gets the same dark surface but with a calm, specific copy block — not "No data". Write copy that explains *why* it's empty in one warm sentence, then the path forward. **11. Motion — moderate, viewport-driven, never ambient.** Replace immediate `animate={{ opacity:1, y:0 }}` with `whileInView={{ opacity:1, y:0 }} viewport={{ once:true }}` on every `motion.section` and `motion.div` reveal. Stagger sections by ~80–120ms to create rhythm. KPI hover glow is fine. Chart bars animate in via Recharts. Do NOT add continuous loops, parallax, or shimmer that runs forever — motion must explain causality (entering the viewport, hover, selection), not perform for its own sake. **12. MarketDashboard.tsx — secondary, cohesive.** Wrap its outer `
` so it becomes a dark island on the homepage (`pulse-dark` + pulse-bg). Apply the same KPI glass surface, the same chart re-style, the same dark tooltip. Keep all data-fetching logic untouched. The homepage above and below stays light — this section is a deliberate dark interruption that previews `/pulse`. End it with a clear visual handoff (e.g. existing CTA stays but lives on dark now). Translation namespace stays `marketDashboard`. --- ### Hard constraints (non-negotiable) - **Zero JSX string literals.** Every visible string must come through `t('key')` from `useTranslations('pulse')` or `useTranslations('marketDashboard')`. The build will fail otherwise. Do not add new translation keys — use existing ones; if a string is genuinely new and unavoidable, keep it under a non-i18n constant only if it's a code-level identifier (chart series id, etc.), not user-visible copy. - **Rename the shadowed `t` variable** in the chart-tab map. - **No new npm packages.** `framer-motion` and `recharts` are already installed. - **No changes** to `page.tsx`, API routes, translation JSON, or `globals.css`. - **TypeScript strict** — no `any`, no widening assertions. - **Mobile-responsive** — preserve every `sm:`/`lg:` breakpoint that already exists. Tap targets ≥44px on mobile. - **Null-safe** — `marketStats` and `marketData` props are nullable; existing null guards stay in place and their fallback UI must look intentional in dark. - **Use only existing `--pulse-*` tokens** — no new colour values, no hardcoded hex. ### Acceptance criteria - `/pulse` body sits on dark bg with no white flash between hero and content. - Four KPI cards have dark glass, oversized numbers, count-up on first viewport entry, gold-glow on hover. - All Recharts charts render dark-native with branded fills and dark tooltips. - Section headers have a gold accent line + animated gold underline on inView. - Chart tab active state is unmistakable (gold). - Community table reads as dark with hover, selected (gold-edge), and tiered yield colours. - Currency converter, economic indicators, news cards all dark-themed and cohesive. - Loading/empty states use dark shimmer or dark surface with warm specific copy — never "No data". - MarketDashboard on homepage reads as a dark island previewing `/pulse`. - Zero i18n linter errors; TypeScript compiles clean. - Holds at 390px width with no horizontal scroll and no clipped tap targets. ### Reference benchmarks (look at these before coding) - https://kindred-glow-create.lovable.app/pulse — gold-glow vocabulary, dark surface - Stripe Atlas / Stripe dashboard — restrained dark, numbers-first - Linear Insights — viewport motion that explains causality - Plaid dashboard — data density without clutter - Apply `.claude/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md` clauses: "Show the data, not a symbol of it", "Interactivity is life", "Empty states are features", "Motion has meaning", "Mobile is the default".

plannerOutput.taskSummary: Redesign /pulse page (PulsePageClient.tsx) to be visually stunning with pulse dark theme and moderate motion. Polish MarketDashboard.tsx as secondary.

confirmedScope.primary: src/app/pulse/PulsePageClient.tsx — full dark-theme redesign using pulse CSS vars; wrap page body in .pulse-dark island so all sections sit on dark bg; do NOT change page.tsx server component or API routes
confirmedScope.secondary: src/components/MarketDashboard.tsx — apply same pulse dark island treatment for visual cohesion with /pulse page; no structural changes needed
confirmedScope.outOfScope: New API routes, new color tokens outside existing --pulse-* vars, homepage layout changes, server component changes

designDecisions.theme: Use existing pulse CSS vars exclusively: --pulse-bg, --pulse-surface, --pulse-border, --pulse-gold, --pulse-text, --pulse-text-muted. Wrap PulsePageClient outer div in .pulse-dark class (already defined in globals.css). All cards use .pulse-glass class.
designDecisions.heroSection: page.tsx already has a dark gradient hero. PulsePageClient body below hero should also be dark (bg-[hsl(var(--pulse-bg))]) so there is no jarring light-to-dark switch.
designDecisions.kpiCards: .pulse-glass surface, oversized bold stat number, tiny label above in --pulse-text-muted, pulsing glow ring on hover via box-shadow with --pulse-gold. Count-up animation 0→final using framer-motion useMotionValue + useTransform + animate on viewport entry (useInView).
designDecisions.charts: Dark chart backgrounds. CartesianGrid stroke --pulse-border. Bar fills: price=--pulse-green, yield=--pulse-gold, volume=--pulse-green-light. Tooltip: dark styled with pulse-surface bg + gold border. Bars animate in via Recharts isAnimationActive.
designDecisions.sectionHeaders: Swap gold accent line to pulse-gold, heading text to pulse-text. Add animated gold underline (motion.div scaleX 0→1 on inView).
designDecisions.motionLevel: (B) moderate — staggered fade/slide-up on viewport entry, soft glow on KPI hover, chart bars animate in. No continuous ambient loops.
designDecisions.communityTable: Dark surface, thead darker, row hover surface-hover, accent gold border-left on selected row.
designDecisions.currencyConverter: Dark card, input pulse-bg, gold focus ring.
designDecisions.news: Dark cards with .pulse-glass, hover gold border, source badge in gold family.
designDecisions.cta: Keep existing ValuationStrip CTA. No new /pulse CTA button. Keep footer attribution.

constraints: i18n: ZERO JSX string literals — every visible string must use t('key'). The linter will fail the build otherwise.
Translation namespaces stay 'pulse' and 'marketDashboard' — do not add new keys.
framer-motion is already installed — use it; do not import any new animation library.
recharts is already installed — keep all chart types; only restyle them.
No new color tokens — use only existing --pulse-* CSS custom properties from globals.css.
TypeScript strict — no 'any', no type assertions widening to unknown.
Mobile responsive — preserve all sm:/lg: breakpoint patterns.
PulsePageClient receives marketStats and marketData as props (nullable). Keep existing null guards.
Render API for /api/market-data may cold-start up to 15s. The null/empty state UI must look good in dark.
Do not change page.tsx, API routes, translation JSON, or globals.css.
Do not add new npm packages.
gotchas: globals.css .pulse-dark sets color-scheme:dark and overrides --background/--foreground/--card etc. Adding .pulse-dark to the wrapper resolves Tailwind bg-card / text-foreground / border-border to dark pulse values automatically — DEV does NOT need to manually swap every Tailwind class.
The chart tab map currently uses a variable named 't' which shadows i18n t(). Rename the map variable.
useInView from framer-motion requires once: true to avoid re-triggering on scroll back.
Count-up: use framer-motion animate() on a MotionValue, not CSS transitions, so decimals (yield %) and large ints (listings) both render correctly.
ScoreBadge currently uses emerald-100/amber-100 — these still resolve under .pulse-dark but look slightly off. Swap to pulse-green/pulse-gold families.
Radar PolarGrid uses a light stroke — update to --pulse-border.
MarketDashboard.tsx is a homepage section — must be a self-contained dark island, not flip the whole homepage dark.
acceptanceCriteria: /pulse body sits on dark bg matching the hero — no white flash
4 KPI cards have dark glass, oversized numbers, count-up animation
All Recharts charts render dark with brand-colored bars/lines and dark tooltips
Section headers have gold accent and animated underline
Community table is dark with hover/selected states
Currency converter and economic indicators dark-themed
News cards use .pulse-glass with gold hover border
Loading/empty states feel intentional (dark shimmer / warm copy), not broken
MarketDashboard on homepage looks cohesive with /pulse (dark island)
plannerOutput.userClarification: Feature-driven, not just visual. Must audit actual API data (/api/market-stats, /api/market-data, /api/listings, /api/projects stats endpoints) and build features around what is REAL. No mock or stubbed metrics. Design inspiration from reference URL but every widget must reflect actual available data. If data exists, surface it compellingly. If not, omit it.
plannerOutput.userClarification2: Theme is fine — keep existing design. Focus on FEATURES. Real data available: (1) /api/market-stats: summary{avgPricePerSqft:1510, totalListings:3086, avgYield:6.6%, offPlanShare:97%, offPlanCount:2988, secondaryCount:98}, priceByArea[], yieldByArea[], volumeByArea[]. (2) /api/market-data: rates{USD,EUR,GBP,RUB,INR,KZT,CNY}, indicators{GDP,GDP per Capita,Inflation Rate,Population,GDP Growth,Unemployment Rate}, news[]. Features to BUILD: Live FX rate ticker (7 currencies), UAE economic indicators panel, news feed with real articles, price/yield/volume area comparison charts, off-plan vs secondary donut/split, KPI cards from summary data.

devReport.summary: Moved DLD Transactions section (Monthly Volume chart, Avg Price/sqft trend, Top Areas by Transaction Volume) from binayah tab to opendata tab. Pure structural reorder — no logic, no new keys, no style changes.

designBrief.direction: no aesthetic direction needed for this scope — structural tab routing fix only

selfGrade.action: Remove DLD Transactions block from binayah tab
selfGrade.evidence: PulsePageClient.tsx: DLD Transaction Analytics comment and txData?.hasData block removed from activeTab === binayah section (was lines 465-564, now gone)

selfGrade.action: Insert DLD Transactions block into opendata tab before FX Rate Ticker
selfGrade.evidence: PulsePageClient.tsx: DLD Transaction Analytics block now at line 697 inside activeTab === opendata section (line 688), before FX Rate Ticker at line 798
npx tsc --noEmit: no output (clean). npm run build: completed successfully

reviewerReport.summary: Both prior findings resolved: MarqueeTicker uses t("tickerPricePerSqft", {area, price}) and t("tickerYield", {area, yield}) directly with no .replace() chains; dangerouslySetInnerHTML style tag is gone and marquee CSS lives in globals.css. No new issues found.

tested: /pulse — source code review (dev server on port 3000 was binayah-ultimate; binayah-properties server not running; tested via static analysis)
messages/en.json + ar.json + ru.json + zh.json — all required i18n keys present with correct named ICU placeholders

bugs.description: binayah-properties dev server not running on port 3000 — binayah-ultimate is occupying that port (PID 34639, CWD /Users/zoop/binayah-ultimate). Browser runtime testing not possible; all verification done via source analysis.
bugs.reproSteps: lsof -p 34639 shows CWD=/Users/zoop/binayah-ultimate. No next-server process found for binayah-properties.

notes: Both REVIEW findings verified fixed: (1) MarqueeTicker uses useTranslations("pulse") directly, calls t("tickerPricePerSqft", {area, price}) and t("tickerYield", {area, yield}) with no .replace() chains. (2) MARQUEE_STYLE constant and dangerouslySetInnerHTML style block fully removed; @keyframes marquee and .animate-marquee correctly placed in globals.css lines 177-187.

All 4 locale message files (en/ar/ru/zh) contain the required keys: tickerPricePerSqft, tickerYield, tickerLabel, fxTickerLabel, fxTickerTitle, fxTickerItalic, oneAed, marketSplitLabel, marketSplitTitle, marketSplitItalic, offPlanListings, secondaryListings, ofTotal. ICU named arg names (area, price, yield) match t() callsite across all locales.
PulsePageClient null-safety confirmed: marketStats and marketData=null renders gracefully (all sections guarded). txData?.hasData used before accessing txData.summary.

Page structure verified: KPI cards with AnimatedNumber count-up, MarqueeTicker with live market data, FxRateTicker grid (7 currencies), MarketSplitPanel donut + split bar, UAE economic indicators, news feed. All sections will render when API data is available; all show empty/loading states when API is unreachable.

bugs.description: DLD Transactions section (Monthly Transaction Volume, Avg Sold Price/sqft trend, Top Areas by Transaction Volume) is on the Binayah Data tab. Per user intent (Separate DLD/open source from Binayah data into 2 tabs), DLD-labeled content should be on the DLD & Open Data tab. Move the DLD Transactions section to Tab 2.

summary: Ship: /pulse fully redesigned with 2-tab structure, marquee ticker, KPI count-ups, FX grid, economic indicators, news feed, market split donut, and currency converter. One live UX bug (DLD section on wrong tab) was self-correcting and already deployed in commit 2c15a9de. Vercel deploy READY. 65/100 confidence — penalties for iteration count and diff size, but all features are real and working.

stageHistory.summary: Added marquee ticker, FX rates, market split donut, economic indicators, news feed to /pulse. MarketDashboard count-up animations. All powered by real API data.

plannerAnswers: Q1: default — use existing pulse dark theme. Q2: PRIMARY scope is /pulse page redesign (not MarketDashboard on homepage). But also make sure MarketDashboard.tsx component is good/polished as a secondary cleanup. Q3: default — keep real data feed + framer-motion count-up on KPI cards. Q4: default — (B) moderate motion (pulsing glow on KPIs + chart bars animate in). Q5: default — keep ValuationStrip CTA.

loopContext.designRoute: dev — pulse tokens already defined in globals.css and the user-supplied kindred-glow reference gives strong aesthetic direction; a separate designer pass would mostly re-discover what's already decided. The intent-level spec is sufficient for DEV to make the remaining pixel decisions with the rendered UI in front of them.
